name: Artemis III
crew: TBD — first woman and first person of colour on Moon
fact: The first crewed lunar landing since Apollo 17 in 1972 — a 55-year gap. Targets the south pole for its water ice deposits, which may enable refuelling for future deep space missions. Will land the first woman and first person of colour on the Moon. If it happens on schedule, it begins the sustained human presence that leads to Mars.
capability: Human polar surface operations and in-situ resource utilisation rehearsal — the direct precursor to crewed Mars missions, which will depend on polar water ice for both consumables and propellant.
